overview: vLLM is an LLM inference engine for high-throughput, low-latency serving of large language models. It supports 200+ model architectures (Llama, Qwen, DeepSeek, Gemma, GPT, etc.), multimodal inputs (image/audio/video), tensor/pipeline parallelism, speculative decoding, quantization (FP8, INT4, AWQ, GPTQ, etc.), PagedAttention-based KV cache management, and a pluggable hardware abstraction layer covering CUDA, ROCm, XPU, TPU, and CPU. The architecture is organized around a V1 engine core (scheduler + executor + worker) with a rich attention backend registry and a compilation pipeline built on torch.compile.

modules.name: V1 Engine Core
modules.description: The main serving pipeline: scheduler (batching, prefill/decode), executor (model execution across devices), worker (device-level model runner), attention backends, sampling/logits processing, KV cache management, speculative decoding, structured output, metrics/logging, and KV offloading. All V1 functionality lives here.

modules.name: Scheduler & KV Cache
modules.description: Core scheduling logic (batching, prefill/decode scheduling, request queuing) and KV cache management (block allocation, prefix caching, cache coordination across PP layers).

modules.name: Attention Backend System
modules.description: Abstract attention backend interface (AttentionBackend) plus 30+ concrete backends spanning FlashAttention, FlashInfer, Triton, FlashMLA, ROCm AITER, FlexAttention, TurboQuant, and platform-specific MLA/sparse MLA variants. Backend selection is cached and delegated to the platform layer.

modules.name: Workers
modules.description: Device-level model runners: GPU worker (with model states, MM encoder runner, speculative decoding runners), CPU worker, XPU worker, TPU model runner. Also handles CUDA graph warmup, LoRA, pipeline parallelism utilities, and encoder CUDA graphs.

modules.name: Executors
modules.description: Abstraction over model execution on one or multiple devices. Supports UniProc (single-process), Multiproc (multi-process), Ray (distributed, both V1 and V2 backends), and external launcher modes. Provides collective_rpc() for worker communication.

modules.name: Engine
modules.description: Top-level engine: LLMEngine (backwards-compatible synchronous API), EngineCore (inner loop with scheduler + executor), CoreClient (IPC client for multi-process mode), InputProcessor/OutputProcessor (request pipeline), and detokenizer/logprob processing.

modules.name: Sampling
modules.description: Token sampling pipeline: logits processing (penalties, bias, min_p), top-k/top-p sampling, bad words filtering, logprob gathering, thinking budget state, and rejection sampling for speculative decoding.

modules.name: KV Cache Spec Interface
modules.description: Defines the KVCacheSpec protocol for describing KV cache layout (FullAttentionSpec, MLAAttentionSpec, MambaSpec, etc.) including page size, block size, dtype, and quantization mode. Used to unify cache layout across backends.

modules.name: Configuration System
modules.description: Comprehensive configuration hierarchy covering model, cache, compilation, device, kernel (IR op priority), load, LoRA, multimodal, parallel (TP/PP/DP), quantization, scheduler, speculative decoding, structured outputs, KV transfer, and more. VllmConfig is the top-level config aggregator.

modules.name: Hardware Platform Abstraction
modules.description: Platform detection and abstraction layer. The Platform base class defines the interface for all hardware backends. Concrete implementations: CudaPlatform (NVIDIA), RocmPlatform (AMD), XPUPlatform (Intel), TpuPlatform (Google), CpuPlatform, ZenCpuPlatform (AMD CPU). Auto-detected via plugin functions in __init__.py. Supports OOT (out-of-tree) platforms.

modules.name: Model Registry & Implementations
modules.description: 200+ model architecture implementations organized as single-file modules. The registry (registry.py) maps HuggingFace config architecture strings to modules. Key base classes: VllmModel, VllmModelForTextGeneration, VllmModelForPooling. Also includes Transformers modeling backend (Base class) that auto-loads HF models and replaces layers with vLLM's TP-aware equivalents.

modules.name: Model Layers & Kernels
modules.description: All model-level neural network layers: attention (PagedAttention, MLA, cross-attention, chunked local, etc.), fused MoE (many expert implementations, routers, oracles), quantization (AWQ, GPTQ, FP8, MXFP4, NVFP4, TurboQuant, etc.), rotary embedding (many variants), Mamba/SSM layers, linear layers, pooler layers, and logits processor. Each layer has multiple kernel backends.

modules.name: Compilation Pipeline
modules.description: Model compilation layer built on torch.compile. CompilerInterface abstraction with InductorAdaptor (PyTorch 2.5-2.7), InductorStandaloneAdaptor (2.8+), and EagerAdaptor (no compile). Includes FX graph passes (fusion for RMSNorm+quant, allreduce+RMSNorm, RoPE+KV cache, MLA dual norm, etc.), CUDA graph support, piecewise compilation, and compilation caching.

modules.name: Serving Entrypoints
modules.description: API serving layer: OpenAI-compatible (chat, completion, models), Anthropic API, MCP tool server, generate/beam search, pooling (classify/embed/score), speech-to-text (realtime/transcription/translation), CLI (serve, benchmark, run_batch), serve-side dev endpoints (cache, RLHF, sleep, RPC, elastic EP), and OpenTelemetry tracing.

modules.name: Distributed Communication
modules.description: Distributed runtime: device communicators (CUDA/NCCL, ROCm, CPU, XPU, custom allreduce, FlashInfer allreduce), parallel state management (TP/PP/DP groups), elastic expert parallelism (EPLB), KV transfer connectors (MoonCake, Nixl, P2P, offloading), weight transfer (IPC, NCCL), and stateless coordinator for dynamic reconfiguration.

modules.name: Plugin System
modules.description: Entry-point-based plugin system supporting general plugins, IO processor plugins, platform plugins, stat logger plugins, and LoRA resolver plugins. Loaded via importlib.metadata.entry_points. Used for OOT platform registration and third-party extensions.

modules.name: DeepSeek V4 Model (Platform-Isolated)
modules.description: DeepSeek V4 architecture with hardware-isolated implementations: common/ (shared ops like rope, cache utils), nvidia/ (FlashMLA-based sparse attention, FlashInfer, CuteDSL kernels), amd/ (ROCm AITER-based kernels), xpu/ (XPU-specific kernels). Runtime dispatch via current_platform flags.

modules.name: Chat Template / Rendering
modules.description: Chat template rendering for converting between API formats and model-native representations. Includes DeepSeek V3/V4, Mistral, Grok, and HuggingFace tokenizer-based renderers.

modules.name: Tokenizer Layer
modules.description: Tokenizer wrappers for DeepSeek V3/V4, Grok, Mistral, Qwen VL, and HuggingFace tokenizers. Handles special token encoding (e.g., DeepSeek V4's encoding scheme).

modules.name: Tool Call Parsers
modules.description: Tool/function-call output parsers for 35+ model families including OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek, Gemini, Mistral, Llama, Qwen, GLM, and more. Both XML-style and Pythonic tool call formats.

modules.name: Reasoning Parsers
modules.description: Reasoning chain extraction parsers for models that emit thinking traces (DeepSeek R1/R1-like, Qwen3, Gemma4, Mistral, Kimi K2, MiniMax M2, etc.). Strips reasoning tokens from final output and tracks thinking budgets.

modules.name: Custom Kernels
modules.description: Platform-specific kernel modules: aiter_ops (ROCm AITER, AMD), helion (custom config/ops), triton (Triton kernels), vllm_c (CUDA C extensions), xpu_ops (Intel XPU), oink_ops.

modules.name: LoRA Adapter System
modules.description: LoRA adapter loading, weight management, and inference. Supports multiple LoRA layers (linear, MoE, embedding, logits processor), multiple kernel backends (Punica, torch ops, Triton ops, XPU ops), and PEFT checkpoint conversion.

modules.name: Multimodal Processing
modules.description: Multimodal input handling: image/audio/video media types, processing pipeline (dummy inputs, budget calculation, EVS token compression), cache management, hashing, and registry.

modules.name: Speculative Decoding
modules.description: Speculative decoding implementations: draft model, Eagle/Eagle3/Medusa, n-gram proposer (CPU/GPU), dFlash, Gemma4 draft, step3.5 lookup, suffix decoding, and rejection sampling.

modules.name: KV Cache Offloading
modules.description: KV cache offloading to CPU memory or tiered storage (filesystem, object store). Includes LRU/ARC eviction policies, swap blocks, and offload workers for GPU-to-CPU transfer.

modules.name: HuggingFace Integration
modules.description: HuggingFace transformers integration: config patching (100+ model-specific configs), processor loading, chat template registry, dynamic module loading, and weight utilities (S3, Run:ai, GGUF).

modules.name: Structured Output
modules.description: Guaranteed structured/JSON output via guidance, LM Format Enforcer, Outlines, and xGrammar backends. Manages grammar state across requests during speculative decoding.

modules.name: Test Suite
modules.description: Comprehensive test suite organized by subsystem: basic_correctness, models (per-model regressions), kernels, distributed, entrypoints, lora, quantization, spec_decode, samplers, multimodal, reasoning, compilation, config, weight_loading, and platform-specific tests (cuda/, rocm/).

key_abstractions.name: Platform
key_abstractions.description: Abstract base class for hardware platform support. Defines all platform-dependent methods: device detection, device capability, attention backend selection, distributed backend, compilation settings, quantization verification, KV cache layout, block size alignment, and IR op priorities. Auto-detected at startup via plugin functions in vllm/platforms/__init__.py.

key_abstractions.name: AttentionBackend
key_abstractions.description: Abstract base class for attention computation backends. Defines the interface for KV cache shape/layout, block size support, dtype/KV cache dtype support, head size support, and forward pass. Concrete implementations validate their configuration against device capability and model parameters.

key_abstractions.name: AttentionBackendEnum
key_abstractions.description: Enumeration of all supported attention backends. Each enum value maps to a fully-qualified class path. Supports runtime overrides via register_backend(). The CUSTOM placeholder enables third-party backends. Selection is cached by (backend, config, num_heads).

key_abstractions.name: Executor
key_abstractions.description: Abstract base class for model execution. Factory method get_class() resolves the backend (ray/mp/uni/external_launcher). Provides collective_rpc() for worker communication. Concrete implementations: UniProcExecutor, MultiprocExecutor, RayDistributedExecutor, RayExecutorV2.

key_abstractions.name: WorkerBase
key_abstractions.description: Abstract base class for device-level workers. Defines lifecycle: init_device(), load_model(), compile_or_warm_up_model(), execute_model(), sample_tokens(). Also handles LoRA management, KV cache spec, and health checks. GPUWorker, CPUWorker, etc. implement this interface.

key_abstractions.name: EngineCore
key_abstractions.description: Inner loop of vLLM's engine. Owns the Executor, Scheduler, KV cache, and structured output manager. Runs the scheduling loop: schedule batches, execute model, process outputs. Communicates with LLMEngine via ZMQ IPC (in multiprocess mode) or direct calls.

key_abstractions.name: LLMEngine
key_abstractions.description: Top-level synchronous engine API. Handles request input processing (via InputProcessor), output processing (via OutputProcessor), engine core communication (via EngineCoreClient), stats logging, and lifecycle management. Can run in single-process or multi-process mode.

key_abstractions.name: VllmConfig
key_abstractions.description: Central configuration object aggregating all sub-configurations: ModelConfig, CacheConfig, ParallelConfig, CompilationConfig, KernelConfig, SchedulerConfig, QuantizationConfig, LoRAConfig, SpeculativeConfig, KVTransferConfig, etc. Created from EngineArgs and passed throughout the system.

key_abstractions.name: VllmModel / VllmModelForTextGeneration
key_abstractions.description: Protocol-based interfaces for all models. VllmModel requires embed_input_ids() and forward(). VllmModelForTextGeneration adds compute_logits(). Models are registered in the registry by HuggingFace architecture name. The Transformers Base class provides automatic HF model loading with vLLM layer replacement.

key_abstractions.name: CompilerInterface
key_abstractions.description: Abstract interface for model compilation. Defines compile() (graph + example_inputs → compiled function + handle) and load() (handle → function). Concrete implementations: InductorAdaptor (PyTorch 2.5-2.7), InductorStandaloneAdaptor (2.8+, supports AOT compilation and binary caching), EagerAdaptor (no compile).

key_abstractions.name: KVCacheSpec / AttentionSpec
key_abstractions.description: Protocol classes describing KV cache layout for a single attention layer. Includes block_size, num_kv_heads, head_size, dtype, kv_quant_mode, and compute page_size_bytes. Variants: FullAttentionSpec, MLAAttentionSpec, MambaSpec, TQFullAttentionSpec. Used to unify cache layout across heterogeneous backends.

module_dependencies: The system follows a layered architecture: (1) Configuration layer (vllm/config/) is loaded first, producing VllmConfig consumed by all components. (2) Platform layer (vllm/platforms/) auto-detects hardware at import time; Platform methods are called throughout the stack for device-specific decisions. (3) Engine layer (vllm/v1/engine/) owns the top-level LLMEngine and EngineCore. (4) Executor layer (vllm/v1/executor/) abstracts model execution - Executor.get_class() resolves the backend. (5) Workers (vllm/v1/worker/) implement device-specific model runners. (6) Attention backends (vllm/v1/attention/) are selected per-layer via the platform's get_attn_backend_cls() and cached. (7) Model implementations (vllm/model_executor/models/) are loaded by the ModelRegistry and use model_executor/layers/ for building blocks. (8) Compilation (vllm/compilation/) wraps torch.compile with custom FX passes and CUDA graph support. (9) Serving entrypoints (vllm/entrypoints/) sit on top of the engine and provide HTTP/gRPC APIs. (10) Distributed communication (vllm/distributed/) is used by executors/workers for TP/PP/DP. (11) Multimodal processing (vllm/multimodal/) feeds into the input processing pipeline. (12) Speculative decoding (vllm/v1/spec_decode/) and LoRA (vllm/lora/) integrate with the worker's model runner.

hardware_abstraction.description: vLLM's hardware abstraction is a multi-layered design. At the top is the Platform base class (vllm/platforms/interface.py) defining ~50 methods covering device detection, attention backend selection, distributed backend, compilation settings, quantization verification, and cache layout. Auto-detection at import time via plugin functions (cuda_platform_plugin, rocm_platform_plugin, xpu_platform_plugin, etc.) or OOT (out-of-tree) plugins via the vllm.platform_plugins entry point. Platform-specific implementations (CudaPlatform, RocmPlatform, XPUPlatform, TpuPlatform, CpuPlatform, ZenCpuPlatform) override base methods. Below the platform layer, attention backends are selected per-model-layer via the platform's get_attn_backend_cls() which respects user overrides, then tries prioritized backends, validating each against compute capability and model config. For models requiring platform-specialized kernels (e.g., DeepSeek V4), the model implementation is fully hardware-isolated: common/ contains shared ops, nvidia/amd/xpu/ contain platform-specific model code and attention backends. The kernel system (vllm/kernels/) provides aiter_ops (ROCm), vllm_c (CUDA), xpu_ops (XPU), and triton (cross-platform) implementations.
platform_independent: Platform base class (vllm/platforms/interface.py) - defines all platform hooks with sensible defaults
AttentionBackend ABC (vllm/v1/attention/backend.py) - abstract interface for all attention implementations
Executor ABC (vllm/v1/executor/abstract.py) - uniform model execution interface
VllmConfig (vllm/config/vllm.py) - unified configuration consumed by all components
Model Registry (vllm/model_executor/models/registry.py) - architecture → model module mapping
Plugin system (vllm/plugins/) - OOT platform and extension registration
EngineCore / LLMEngine (vllm/v1/engine/) - scheduling loop and API
platform_specific: CudaPlatform (vllm/platforms/cuda.py) - NVIDIA GPU support with NVML-based detection and NCCL backend
RocmPlatform (vllm/platforms/rocm.py) - AMD GPU support with ROCm backend
XPUPlatform (vllm/platforms/xpu.py) - Intel XPU support with XCCL backend
ZenCpuPlatform (vllm/platforms/zen_cpu.py) - AMD Zen CPU with zentorch
Platform-specific attention backends: FlashInfer (NVIDIA), FlashMLA (NVIDIA), ROCm AITER (AMD), XPU MLA Sparse (Intel)
Platform-specific model implementations: DeepSeek V4's nvidia/amd/xpu/ subdirectories with fully separate model classes and MTP implementations
Platform-specific kernels: aiter_ops (ROCm), vllm_c (CUDA), xpu_ops (XPU), helion (custom), triton (cross-platform)
Platform-specific communicators: CudaCommunicator, CpuCommunicator, XpuCommunicator, RayCommunicator
Platform-specific CUDA graph wrappers, distributed backends (NCCL, XCCL, Gloo), and quantization verification

test_structure.description: Tests are organized by subsystem mirroring the source structure. Root conftest.py provides shared fixtures (model loading, vLLM config creation, temporary directories). Subdirectories: basic_correctness/ (end-to-end correctness), models/ (per-model regression tests with example HF models in registry), kernels/ (kernel-level unit tests), distributed/ (TP/PP/DP integration), entrypoints/ (API serving tests), lora/ (LoRA adapter loading/inference), quantization/ (per-method quantization tests), spec_decode/ (speculative decoding), samplers/ (sampling logic), multimodal/ (image/audio/video), reasoning/ (reasoning parser tests), compile/ (torch.compile integration), config/ (configuration parsing and validation), engine/ (engine lifecycle), weight_loading/ (weight loader tests), and platform-specific cuda/ and rocm/ directories. Standalone tests simulate multi-GPU scenarios without actual GPUs.
